mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in industrial production signals improving operating leverage and near-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value available for 2006-05-01 was 112.0991
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO momentum does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.75%, tighter discount-rate conditions reduce equity valuation support and predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening household demand, construction activity, and credit-sensitive consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing weakness is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside hit rate is not above baseline by at least 10 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with falling housing starts identify a tightening shock hitting rate-sensitive demand, which compresses expected earnings and predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with housing weakness
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not underperform either single-series trigger, or if average 60-day S&P 500 returns after the joint trigger are not negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth with a stable unemployment rate signals durable income growth without immediate labor-market deterioration, supporting consumption and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE 3-month change <= 0.1pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations fail to exceed unconditional 20-day S&P 500 returns by at least 25 basis points on average or if positive-return frequency is below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI inflation raises the expected path of policy rates and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days around inflation-sensitive repricing windows.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI and FEDFUNDS joint trigger is not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than baseline, or if its mean return is positive across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth exceeding CPI inflation indicates positive real consumption momentum, improving revenue expectations and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds CPIAUCSL 3-month growth by > 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if real retail-sales momentum has Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window or if triggered returns do not exceed baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and recession-risk repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days after the labor deterioration becomes visible.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
regime_hint: transition from expansion to labor-market slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if drawdown frequency is not above the unconditional rate.
